headline: Record chip rally adds $2T to Micron, Intel, AMD in Q2
detail: Semiconductors just had one of the strongest quarters on record, directly boosting SMH, SOXL, and SPAL in your book. The key risk now is that extended rallies after a massive run often invite sharp pullbacks, especially in leveraged names. This is a reason to stay disciplined on stops, not to add aggressively.
rec: No new buys off this headline alone - your chip exposure via SMH, SOXL, and SPAL already captures this. Focus on protecting gains with stops rather than chasing a name that just had its best quarter in years.

headline: Investors piled into ETFs at record pace in H1 2026
detail: Record ETF inflows signal strong retail and institutional appetite for broad market and sector exposure - directly relevant since most of your book is ETF-based (QQQ, SPY, SMH, SOXL, QQQI, SPAL). Heavy inflows can sustain momentum but also mean crowded trades that unwind fast. Your leveraged ETF names are especially vulnerable to a crowded-trade reversal.
rec: No action needed today, but be aware your leveraged positions sit in one of the most crowded trades of the year - tighten mental stops and don't add size into strength.

headline: Small-caps enjoy best first half in 35 years
detail: The IWM/small-cap surge supports the broad risk-on environment that is keeping your QQQ and SPY afloat. It also means market breadth is healthy, which is a good backdrop for tech. However, this is largely a macro tailwind, not a direct catalyst for your chip-heavy names.
rec: No action - your QQQ and SPY are already benefiting from this environment and are solidly in the green. The IWM scanner setup (7/12, grade A) is noted but not compelling enough on its own to add a new position when you're already long the market through QQQ and SPY.

headline: Trump discloses expanding financial empire including crypto
detail: Crypto and political headlines can spark short-term volatility in risk assets and sentiment-driven tech names. No direct impact on your chip or ETF holdings from this disclosure. Worth monitoring only if broader risk sentiment shifts.
rec: Nothing actionable here for your book - skip it and stay focused on chip price action and your leveraged stop levels.
